title: राधा कृपा कटाक्ष स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राधा कृपा कटाक्ष स्तोत्रम् ॥

मुनीन्द्रवृन्दवन्दिते त्रिलोकशोकहारिणी
प्रसन्नवक्त्रपंकजे निकुंजभूविलासिनी।
व्रजेन्द्रभानुनन्दिनी व्रजेन्द्रसूनुसंगते
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ १॥

अशोकवृक्षवल्लरी वितानमण्डपस्थिते
प्रवालबालपल्लवाभरणशोभिते।
समस्तवैष्णवीजने विशेषतदर्शिते
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ २॥

अनन्तकोटिविष्णुलोकनामवृन्दवन्दिते
हरिप्रियावदन्तवत्सलत्वविख्यातिविश्रुते।
हरिप्रियार्चिते सदा नवीनकुंकुमाङ्किते
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ३॥

समस्तसौभगाल्पसन्निहितसद्गुणोज्ज्वले
कृष्णप्रेमपरम्परासुलभसन्दृशे।
लतालुलितगात्रि गोकुलप्रियंकरे
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ४॥

ललाटचम्पकोत्तमोज्ज्वलं समुद्यते
त्रिरेखललितालकालिभाल हारशोभिते।
सुवर्णमौलिमण्डने महारत्नकुण्डले
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ५॥

सुमन्त्रमोहनौषधं मृगाक्षपेक्षणं
महापुरुषलक्षणं समस्तोल्बणम्।
नवप्रेमधाम्नि सन्दृशं सुखावहं
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ६॥

वृन्दावनविलासिनी प्रियसखीमनोहरे
व्रजेश्वरी मनस्विनी प्रसिद्धदिव्यवैभवे।
गोवर्धने गदाधरं मुदा प्रियंकरे
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ७॥

निकुञ्जकान्तरासरासविलासिनी
मुरारिकामदायिनी मनोगतप्रदायिनी।
नमामि ते पदाम्बुजद्वयं सदा शुचिस्मिते
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ८॥

जगन्मनोऽभिरामया सदा कृपावलोकया
स्वभक्तपक्षपातिन्या रसार्द्रया दृशा।
भवत्यधीनभक्तया सदा विभूषया
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ ९॥

गतातिविस्तरस्तव प्रियो हि राधिके सदा
मुदा त्वयाऽभिरक्षितस्तव प्रभावतो जगत्।
त्वदीयपादपद्मसेवनेच्छयाखिलस्त्वहं
कदा करिष्यसीह मां कृपाकटाक्षभाजनम्॥ १०॥

हा देवि काकु भरवाक्यैः प्रसीद केवलम्।
त्वयी श्रद्धा सदा स्यान्मे गोविन्ददयिते नमः॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री ऊर्ध्वाम्नायतन्त्रे श्री राधा कृपा कटाक्ष स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram ||

Munindra-vrinda-vandite tri-loka-shoka-harini
Prasanna-vaktra-pankaje nikunja-bhu-vilasini.
Vrajendra-bhanu-nandini Vrajendra-sunu-sangate
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 1 ||

Ashoka-vriksha-vallari vitana-mandapa-sthite
Pravala-bala-pallava-abharana-shobhite.
Samasta-Vaishnavi-jane vishesha-tad-arshite
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 2 ||

Ananta-koti-Vishnu-loka-nama-vrinda-vandite
Hari-priya-avadanta-vatsalatva-vikhyati-vishrute.
Hari-priya-archite sada navina-kunkuma-ankite
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 3 ||

Samasta-saubhagalpa-sannihita-sad-guna-ujjvale
Krishna-prema-parampara-sulabha-san-drishe.
Lata-lulita-gatri Gokula-priyankare
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 4 ||

Lalata-champaka-uttama-ujjvalam samudyate
Tri-rekha-lalita-alaka-alibhala-hara-shobhite.
Suvarna-mauli-mandane maha-ratna-kundale
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 5 ||

Su-mantra-mohana-aushadham mriga-aksha-pekshanam
Maha-purusha-lakshanam samasta-ulbanam.
Nava-prema-dhamni sandrisham sukha-avaham
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 6 ||

Vrindavana-vilasini priya-sakhi-manohare
Vrajeshvari manasvini prasiddha-divya-vaibhave.
Govardhane Gadadharam muda priyankare
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 7 ||

Nikunja-kanta-rasa-rasa-vilasini
Murari-kama-dayini mano-gata-pradayini.
Namami te pada-ambuja-dvayam sada shuchi-smite
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 8 ||

Jagan-mano-abhiramaya sada kripa-avalokaya
Sva-bhakta-paksha-patinya rasa-ardraya drisha.
Bhavaty-adhina-bhaktaya sada vibhushaya
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 9 ||

Gata-ati-vistaras-tava priyo hi Radhike sada
Muda tvaya-abhirakshitas-tava prabhavato jagat.
Tvadiya-pada-padma-sevana-ichchhaya-akhilas-tvaham
Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam. || 10 ||

Ha Devi kaku bhara-vakyaih prasida kevalam.
Tvayi shraddha sada syan-me Govinda-dayite namah. || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Urdhvamnaya-Tantre Shri Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मुनीन्द्रों के समूह द्वारा वन्दित, तीनों लोकों के शोक को हरने वाली, प्रसन्न मुखकमल वाली, निकुञ्ज भूमि में विलास करने वाली, वृषभानुनन्दिनी, व्रजेन्द्रसूनु (कृष्ण) से संगत — हे राधे! कब आप मुझे अपनी कृपा-कटाक्ष का पात्र बनाएँगी?॥ १॥

अशोक वृक्ष की लताओं से बने मण्डप में स्थित, प्रवाल-किसलय के आभूषणों से शोभित, समस्त वैष्णवजनों में विशेष रूप से दर्शित — कब मुझे कृपा-कटाक्ष का पात्र बनाएँगी?॥ २॥

वृन्दावन-विलासिनी, प्रिय सखियों को मनोहर, व्रजेश्वरी, मनस्विनी, प्रसिद्ध दिव्य वैभव वाली, गोवर्धन पर गदाधर (कृष्ण) को प्रसन्न करने वाली — कब मुझे कृपा-कटाक्ष का पात्र बनाएँगी?॥ ७॥

जगत् के मन को मोहित करने वाली, सदा कृपा दृष्टि से देखने वाली, अपने भक्तों का पक्ष लेने वाली, रसार्द्र दृष्टि वाली — कब मुझे कृपा-कटाक्ष का पात्र बनाएँगी?॥ ९॥

हे देवि! करुण वाक्यों से प्रसन्न हों! आपमें मेरी श्रद्धा सदा बनी रहे। हे गोविन्ददयिते! आपको नमस्कार॥ ११॥
benefits: Invokes the merciful glance (kripa-kataksha) of Shri Radha
Radha is the supreme shakti of Krishna — her grace grants Krishna-prema
Extremely sacred stotra from the Urdhvamnaya Tantra tradition
Invokes entry into the divine pastimes of vrindavana
Invokes the blessing of pure devotional love (prema-bhakti) through radha's mercy
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles on the path of bhakti
description: Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram — राधा कृपा कटाक्ष स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Radha. Rooted in Urdhvamnaya Tantra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow pure devotional love (prema-bhakti) through Radha's mercy and grant entry into the divine pastimes of Vrindavana. It is also said to remove all obstacles on the path of bhakti. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Radha Ashtami, Janmashtami, and Holi. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer seeking the merciful glance of Shri Radha, queen of Vrindavana.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram and what does the title mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram (राधा कृपा कटाक्ष स्तोत्रम्) is one of the most beloved Vrindavan devotional hymns to Sri Radha Rani, preserved in the classical Urdhvamnaya Tantra. The Sanskrit title decomposes as: Radha — the supreme consort of Sri Krishna, the very embodiment of his ahladini shakti; kripa — divine grace, mercy; kataksha — the side-glance, the merciful glance from the corner of the eye (a precious devotional concept signifying that even a fleeting glance of the Devi grants total spiritual fulfilment); stotram — hymn of praise. Together: 'A hymn praying for the merciful glance of Sri Radha.' Each of the eleven verses ends with the iconic refrain Kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam — 'When, O Radhe, will you make me a recipient of your merciful glance?' (Sources: Urdhvamnaya Tantra; Vrindavan parampara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous opening 'Munindra vrinda vandite tri-loka-shoka-harini'?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse — Munindra-vrinda-vandite tri-loka-shoka-harini, prasanna-vaktra-pankaje nikunja-bhu-vilasini, Vrajendra-bhanu-nandini Vrajendra-sunu-sangate, kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam — invokes Sri Radha through her most exalted titles. Munindra-vrinda-vandite: O you who are venerated by the assembly of the foremost munis (Sanaka, Sanatana, Narada, Vyasa, Shukadeva); tri-loka-shoka-harini: remover of the sorrows of the three worlds; prasanna-vaktra-pankaje: with a serene lotus-face; nikunja-bhu-vilasini: who delights in the kunja-groves of Vrindavan; Vrajendra-bhanu-nandini: daughter of Vrishabhanu, king of Vraja; Vrajendra-sunu-sangate: ever in the company of the son of Nanda Maharaja (Krishna); kada karishyasiha: when will you indeed make; mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam: me a recipient of your merciful glance. (Sources: Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Sri Radha and what is her significance in Krishna bhakti?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Radha is the supreme consort of Sri Krishna, the daughter of Vrishabhanu Maharaja and Kirtida in Barsana of Vraja-mandala. In the Vaishnava theological traditions of Gaudiya, Pushtimarga, Nimbarka and Radha-vallabha sampradayas, Radha is not merely a beloved gopi but the very ahladini shakti (bliss-power) of Krishna himself — the feminine half of the non-dual Para Brahman, without whom Krishna himself cannot be fully Krishna (as verse 2 of the Radha Stotram declares: 'You are dearer to Krishna than his own pranas; without you Govinda himself does not shine'). In the Urdhvamnaya Tantra and Brahma Vaivarta Purana, Radha is identified as Mula Prakriti, the original Devi, of whom Lakshmi, Saraswati, Durga and all goddesses are expansions. The bhakta therefore approaches Krishna only through the mediation of Radha's kripa. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Urdhvamnaya Tantra; Gaudiya Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual significance of seeking Radha's kripa-kataksha?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Radha-Krishna bhakti tradition, the kripa-kataksha (merciful side-glance) of Sri Radha is considered the supreme blessing — even greater than the direct grace of Krishna himself. The reason given by the Vrindavan acharyas is that Krishna gives liberation (mukti), but Radha alone gives prema-bhakti — the highest goal beyond even mukti. Krishna may be approached by the upasaka through worship and dharma, but his innermost lila in the kunjas of Vrindavan is accessible only through Radha's grace, for Radha is the ashraya (refuge) of bhakti while Krishna is the vishaya (object). The eleven repetitions of kada karishyasiha mam kripa-kataksha-bhajanam are therefore not mere requests but the entire saranagati of the bhakta — surrendering all sadhana and seeking only Radha's merciful glance as the door to Krishna-prema. (Sources: classical Vrindavan bhakti tradition; Gaudiya theology.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram is most powerful when recited at Brahma muhurta or in the sandhya hours, after bath, before a Sri Radha-Krishna or Sri Radha murti/picture, on a clean asana, with deep contemplation on the divine form of Radha being praised in each verse. It is especially auspicious on Wednesdays (Budhvar — sacred to Krishna and his beloved Radha), Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to the Devi), every Ekadashi, on Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — the supreme appearance day of Sri Radha at Barsana), Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — Krishna's appearance), Sharad Purnima (the rasa-lila night), Govardhana Puja, Holi (Krishna's lila day), and throughout the four months of Chaturmasya. Devotees on Vraja-mandala parikrama recite it especially at Barsana, Nandgaon, Sanket, Radha Kund and Vrindavan. (Sources: Vrindavan parikrama paddhati; Vaishnava recitation tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Vrindavan acharyas attribute the following benefits to regular recitation of the Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram with devotion: awakening of pure prema-bhakti (divine love) for Sri Radha-Krishna, the supreme goal of Vaishnava sadhana; entry into the divine kunja-lilas of Vrindavan through Radha's grace; removal of all obstacles on the path of bhakti, especially aparadhas (offences) committed against vaishnavas; protection of the bhakta in this life through Radha's maternal love (sva-bhakta-paksha-patinya as verse 9 declares); fulfilment of all manogata desires (especially desires for darshan and seva of the divine couple); attainment of bhava and the final goal of Goloka-vasa with eternal seva of Sri Radha-Madhava; and the supreme phala — Radha's prasanna-darshan and the awakening of Krishna-prema as taught by all Vrindavan acharyas. (Sources: Urdhvamnaya Tantra; Gaudiya bhakti tradition.)
